skill.name: pattern-detection
skill.description: Detect patterns, anomalies, and trends in code and data. Use when identifying code smells, finding security vulnerabilities, or discovering recurring patterns. Handles regex patterns, AST analysis, and statistical anomaly detection.
skill.summary: Detect patterns, anomalies, and trends in code and data using regex, statistical analysis, and code structure patterns.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 36 patterns (external_commands: 28, network: 3, env_access: 1), but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The SKILL.md file is documentation containing example code snippets in markdown code blocks - not executable code. The 'backtick execution' patterns are bash examples in fenced code blocks showing how to build pattern detection tools. The URLs are documentation references. The env_access flag detected a grep pattern for finding hardcoded secrets - this is instructional content, not actual credential access. No actual security risks exist.

evidence.reason: Markdown code block showing example bash command - not executable
Documentation example of grep patterns for code analysis
Example regex pattern in markdown - not executable code
SQL injection detection examples in documentation
Pattern examples for detecting hardcoded secrets - instructional only
Examples showing dangerous function detection - not actual dangerous usage
Error handling pattern examples in documentation
Python code snippet showing regex pattern matching - documentation only
Trend analysis Python example - not executable by skill
Reference URL to regex101.com in documentation - not a network call
Reference URL to OWASP cheatsheet - documentation link only
Reference URL to refactoring.guru - documentation link only
Grep pattern for detecting hardcoded secrets in other files - not actual credential access

content.user_title: Detect code patterns, anomalies, and security vulnerabilities
content.value_statement: Identify code smells, security vulnerabilities, and problematic patterns in your codebase. This skill provides systematic pattern detection using regex, statistical analysis, and code structure analysis to improve code quality and security posture.
actual_capabilities: Detect code smells like long functions, duplicate code, and magic numbers
Scan for security vulnerabilities including SQL injection, hardcoded secrets, and dangerous function usage
Identify statistical anomalies in data using z-score and IQR methods
Analyze code structure patterns including imports, TODO markers, and error handling
Generate comprehensive pattern detection reports with severity rankings
limitations: Read-only analysis - cannot automatically fix detected issues
May produce false positives requiring manual review
Pattern matching based - may miss context-specific issues
Limited to text-based pattern detection - no AST parsing for all languages

use_cases.title: Code Review Automation
use_cases.description: Proactively scan pull requests for code quality issues, security vulnerabilities, and maintainability concerns before human review.
use_cases.target_user: Development teams implementing automated code review workflows

use_cases.title: Security Audit Scanning
use_cases.description: Identify potential security vulnerabilities like hardcoded credentials, SQL injection patterns, and dangerous function usage across large codebases.

use_cases.title: Technical Debt Assessment
use_cases.description: Quantify code quality issues and technical debt by detecting code smells, TODO markers, and problematic patterns for refactoring prioritization.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Pattern Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan my codebase for common code smells and security vulnerabilities. Focus on: 1) Long functions over 50 lines, 2) Hardcoded secrets or API keys, 3) SQL injection patterns. Report findings with file paths and line numbers.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick security and quality check on a new codebase

prompt_templates.title: Deep Security Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform comprehensive security pattern detection on the /src directory. Check for: hardcoded credentials, SQL injection risks, eval/exec usage, XSS patterns (innerHTML), and command injection. For each finding, include severity level, file location, and recommended fix.

prompt_templates.title: Code Duplication Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Find duplicate and similar code patterns across my project. Look for: 1) Copy-pasted code blocks (3+ identical lines), 2) Similar conditional patterns, 3) Repeated error handling. Group by similarity and suggest consolidation opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Statistical Anomaly Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this dataset for statistical anomalies using z-score and IQR methods. Flag data points with z-score > 3 or outside IQR bounds. Provide visual summary of anomaly distribution and potential root causes.

output_examples.input: Scan for security vulnerabilities in /api directory
output_examples.output: Pattern Detection Report - Security vulnerabilities (HIGH): Found 3 hardcoded API keys in config.js:42, database.py:15, and utils.ts:88. Found 2 SQL injection patterns in handlers/user.js:56 and queries/product.ts:23. Recommended actions: 1) Move secrets to environment variables, 2) Use parameterized queries, 3) Review error handling in affected files.

Pattern Detection Report - Code smells (MEDIUM): Found 5 functions exceeding 50 lines (longest: processData at 147 lines). Found 12 magic numbers without named constants. Found 3 empty catch blocks. Found 8 TODO/FIXME markers. Recommended actions: 1) Extract large functions into smaller units, 2) Define named constants for magic numbers, 3) Implement proper error handling.

best_practices: Start with high-severity patterns first (security vulnerabilities) before code smells
Review findings in context to distinguish false positives from real issues
Document detection patterns for consistent team-wide code quality standards
anti_patterns: Do not auto-fix detected issues without human review - context matters
Avoid flagging every pattern match as a bug - some patterns are intentional
Do not scan binary files or generated code - results will be noisy

faq.question: What file types does this skill support?
faq.answer: Pattern detection works best on source code files including JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, Java, Ruby, and other text-based languages. Binary files and generated code are excluded.

faq.question: Can this skill automatically fix detected issues?
faq.answer: No, this is a read-only analysis skill. It detects and reports patterns but does not modify code. Fixes should be implemented after human review to ensure correctness.

faq.question: How accurate is the pattern detection?
faq.answer: Pattern detection uses regex and heuristics which can produce false positives. All findings should be reviewed in context. Security findings typically have higher confidence than code smell detection.

faq.question: Can I customize the patterns being detected?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify custom patterns, file filters, and severity thresholds in your prompts. The skill adapts to your specific code quality and security requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill work with private repositories?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes files accessible in the current workspace. For private repositories, ensure the AI tool has appropriate access to scan the codebase.

faq.question: What is the performance impact on large codebases?
faq.answer: Pattern detection uses efficient grep and regex operations. For very large codebases (100k+ lines), consider scanning specific directories or file types to reduce processing time.
